title_original: Demonstration of high-fidelity entangled logical qubits using transmons | Nature Communications
summary_original: A team led by Arian Vezvaee and Daniel A. Lidar reports a method combining error correction codes with dynamical decoupling, called normalizer dynamical decoupling, to significantly suppress logical errors in quantum computers. Using IBM transmon devices and the [[4, 2, 2]] code, they demonstrate entangled logical Bell states with fidelities that "significantly exceed" those of unprotected qubits. The ability to detect logical errors—mainly from physical qubit crosstalk—represents a notable advance. These results suggest a practical step forward toward fault-tolerant quantum computing, as the demonstrated logical qubit performance is "beyond-breakeven" for current devices. All experimental data and code are available from the authors upon request.
